title: triticum aestivum var. dicoccum
intro: Triticum aestivum var. dicoccum, also known as emmer wheat, is a type of ancient wheat that is an heirloom variety of wheat with a high nutritional value. It is rich in minerals such as iron, zinc, and selenium, and has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of emmer wheat include lowering cholesterol levels, improving blood sugar control, and providing relief from symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Emmer wheat can be prepared in a variety of ways, including as a flour for baking, as a breakfast porridge, or as a tea by infusing the grains in hot water.
